purpose: Define the exact scalar quantity that must be extracted from the Lemma 5.9 source trace in order to convert the current lower-root structural information into a theorem-grade source coefficient.

target_quantity.name: kappa_src^(i)
target_quantity.definition: For each component Sigma_i, define kappa_src^(i) by ev_{-1/2}^{(i)}(m(h_bar)) = (c0^(i))^2 * kappa_src^(i) * Phi_{-1/2}^{std} + higher / s-dependent corrections, where Phi_{-1/2}^{std} is the fixed sigma-odd mu=-1/2 normal form on the collar.
target_quantity.normal_form_basis: Phi_{-1/2}^{std} = the chosen real two-dimensional basis of the mu=-1/2 sigma-odd trace space, to be fixed consistently with Theorem 5.10 / R^{2N} coordinates.

source_side.from_draft: ev_{-1/2}(m(h_bar)) is s-uniform at leading order and is sourced by the quadratic remainder only.

channel_side.from_rho2_scaffold: The branch-motion normal form has exact lower-root coefficient 3/8 in the mu=-1/2 channel.

comparison_problem.what_must_be_computed: Express the leading source trace ev_{-1/2}^{(i)}(m(h_bar)) in the same basis Phi_{-1/2}^{std} used for the branch-motion normal form.
comparison_problem.why_this_is_the_right_target: Once kappa_src^(i) is known, the collar source contribution becomes an explicit scalar multiple of the already rigidified lower-root channel, and the remaining source bound reduces to coefficient size plus the O(rho^(3/2)) tail.

what_is_not_fixed_yet: The explicit choice of Phi_{-1/2}^{std} in repository coordinates.
The scalar value or interval for kappa_src^(i).
